files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,96 @@
+# Real-Use Smoke Suite Design
+
+## Goal
+
+Add a deterministic smoke suite that validates real Ghost Pepper operator journeys against a built macOS app bundle. The suite must exercise LaunchServices startup, menu bar interaction, window opening, and crash detection instead of relying only on in-process unit tests.
+
+## Why This Exists
+
+Recent regressions escaped the hosted test suite because they only reproduced in the live menu bar app:
+
+- onboarding launched with the wrong side effects and crashed before the expected first-run flow
+- status menu actions trapped on actor isolation when clicked from the real menu bar menu
+
+The repository already contains one smoke script for onboarding. This work expands that into a small, repeatable suite with a single entry point.
+
+## Constraints
+
+- The suite must launch a real `.app` bundle.
+- The suite must drive UI through macOS automation that works with a menu bar app.
+- The suite must not reuse Jesse's everyday preferences or application-support files.
+- The suite should avoid flake from model downloads, remote services, or long-running runtime behavior unless those paths are explicitly stabilized later.
+
+## Recommended Approach
+
+Implement the smoke suite as shell scripts under `scripts/`, using `open`, `osascript`, `pgrep`, and crash-report comparisons.
+
+The runner will:
+
+1. build a fresh smoke app into isolated derived data
+2. override the app bundle identifier at build time so TCC and defaults are scoped to the smoke app
+3. launch the app with an isolated `HOME` so `~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper` and related user-scoped state stay out of the normal account data
+4. run a series of named smoke journeys
+5. report pass or fail per journey and return a nonzero exit on any failure
+
+## Initial Journeys
+
+### Onboarding first run
+
+Validate that a first-run launch shows onboarding and does not trigger an early microphone permission sheet before the onboarding window is ready.
+
+### Onboarding-only menu
+
+Validate that the pre-onboarding menu contains only:
+
+- `Show Setup Window`
+- `Quit`
+
+### Settings window
+
+Validate that a post-onboarding launch can open `Settings...` from the real menu bar menu, that the `Ghost Pepper Settings` window appears, and that no crash report is generated.
+
+### Debug log window
+
+Validate that a post-onboarding launch can open `Debug Log...` from the real menu bar menu and that the process remains alive.
+
+### Quit action
+
+Validate that selecting `Quit` from the menu bar menu terminates the launched smoke app process.
+
+## Isolation Strategy
+
+The suite uses two forms of isolation:
+
+### Bundle identity isolation
+
+The smoke runner builds Ghost Pepper with a smoke-only bundle identifier, for example `com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper.smoke`. This prevents TCC resets and prompts from colliding with the normal installed app.
+
+### Home directory isolation
+
+The smoke runner launches the app through `open --env HOME=...`, pointing at a temporary directory. This keeps defaults, persistent UI state, and application-support writes out of Jesse's normal home directory state.
+
+## Shared Harness
+
+The suite should expose a small shared shell library for:
+
+- locating and validating the built app bundle
+- building the smoke app with the alternate bundle identifier
+- launching a fresh app instance and capturing the new PID
+- querying or clicking menu bar items through `osascript`
+- comparing the latest `GhostPepper-*.ips` report before and after a test
+- cleaning up temporary homes and app instances
+
+Each journey script should stay small and focus on one behavior, while the shared harness owns the common process and UI plumbing.
+
+## Non-Goals For This Pass
+
+- validating full onboarding completion through live model downloads
+- validating transcription quality, cleanup output, or OCR
+- validating system permission panes beyond detecting early unexpected prompts
+- replacing hosted unit tests
+
+Those paths are either already covered elsewhere or are too nondeterministic to count as trustworthy smoke validation without additional fixtures.
+
+## Success Criteria
+
+This design is successful when Jesse can run one command locally and get a clear pass or fail result for the deterministic real-use shell journeys that have recently regressed in production-like runs.
